title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Query Trace: Trigger Audit Test
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql trigger audit test increment in a versioned read model and audited transition design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test trigger side effects, recursion guards, changed rows, rollback, and accessible audit evidence.
Place constraints, derived data, transitions, and orchestration in database or application layers deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 103. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects. A defensible solution applies Trigger Audit Test, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.code:
CREATE TEMP VIEW open_ticket_queue AS
SELECT ticket_id, team_id, owner_id, severity, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved';
SELECT ticket_id, severity
FROM open_ticket_queue
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-trigger-audit-test-17floc" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.
